value_json.description: A tenant must give a completed copy of the entry condition report back to the landlord/agent within 7 days of being given possession of the property.

When a bond claim is lodged via Rental Bonds Online, NSW Fair Trading issues a Notice of Claim by email/SMS. The other party has 14 days to accept or dispute it. If undisputed, Fair Trading pays the claim after 14 days.
To dispute a bond claim, an application to NCAT must be lodged within the same 14-day notice period. The bond is not released until the dispute is resolved or an NCAT order is made.

NCAT Consumer and Commercial Division residential proceedings fee, effective 1 July 2026. Concession rate (25% of standard) applies to eligible pensioners, legal aid recipients, or those assisted by a community legal centre.

value_json.principle: A tenant is never liable for fair wear and tear — the normal deterioration of a property from ordinary use, exposure to the elements, and time. A tenant is responsible for negligent, irresponsible, or intentional damage.

categories.wear_and_tear: Furniture indentations and traffic marks; sun-fading
categories.damage: Stains or burn marks on the carpet
Badly scratched or gouged wooden floors

categories.wear_and_tear: Faded, chipped, or cracked paint; paint worn off near light switch
categories.damage: Unapproved, poor quality paint job; damage from removing posters stuck with blu-tack or tape

categories.wear_and_tear: Loose hinges or handles; worn sliding tracks
Stains from a leaking roof or bad plumbing (not tenant-caused)
categories.damage: Stains from an overflowing bath or indoor pot plants
